title: lastrea subdimorpha
intro: Lastrea subdimorpha, also known as the Dimorphic Lady Fern, is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is a type of vascular plant that is native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia and the Philippines. The medicinal properties of Lastrea subdimorpha include its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and boosting the immune system, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use.
